issues.Detail: Note does not meet NDIS progress note requirements. Issues: Note is truncated/incomplete - ends mid-sentence: 'she appeared happy, relaxed, and excited. After returning home, Nicole wanted support with cleaning and tidying the house. We worked together to complete the household tasks while she listened to her favourite music on the TV, which helped create a positive and enjoyable atmosphere. Nicole then decided she needed to withdraw some money, so we went to Broadmeadows Shopping Centre. After completing this task, we returned home and began preparing dinner together. Nicole wanted to cook meatballs and actively participated in the meal preparation. During cooking, she realised she had forgotten to buy parsley and a coat hanger, so we made another quick trip to the shops to purchase the items she needed. Once back home, Nicole happily continued cooking and completed the meal. She was in a great mood, engaged well in all activities, and showed good motivation throughout the day. After dinner preparation, we went for a pleasant walk together, which Nicole enjoyed. We then returned home and worked together to wash and put away the dishes. Overall, Nicole had a very positive and productive day. She actively participated in community acces' - final word cut off; Excessive detail and repetitive structure suggests AI-generation or templating; Overly positive tone throughout without any neutral observations or areas for development; Shift duration 7.1 hours with extensive activities listed - no clear time breakdown makes verification difficult; Cannot properly assess completion of note due to truncation
